_note: Paths here point at samples/ — drop your own short/long screen recordings there (gitignored) to reproduce the eval loop. See docs/evals.md.

evals.name: visual-summary
evals.prompt: What's happening in this screen recording? Give me a quick rundown. samples/short-screen-recording.mp4
evals.expected_output: Probes the clip, runs an estimate, extracts frames (visual tier — short screen recording), Reads them, and returns a grounded TL;DR + [MM:SS] beats describing what's on screen. No spend (local visual). Should note the screen is near-static if so.

evals.name: audio-doc
evals.prompt: Transcribe this meeting and save me notes I can keep: samples/short-screen-recording.mp4
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the value is in the audio, transcribes with the local faster-whisper backend ($0, no upload), and writes a durable note to the workspace out_dir (<stem>.md) with a TL;DR + [MM:SS] beats. Reports the saved path. No out-of-pocket spend.

evals.name: cost-gate-skip
evals.prompt: Before you do anything, estimate what it'd cost to transcribe and summarize this ~13-minute recording so I can decide if it's worth it: samples/long-screen-recording.mp4
evals.expected_output: Probes + runs estimate ONLY. Presents the cost breakdown (transcription $ vs agent token $, dominant driver) and a clear go/skip recommendation with cheaper levers. Does NOT extract frames or transcribe — waits for the user to decide.
